linting.notes: No linter configuration found. No shellcheck configuration, pre-commit hooks, or automated shell script validation. Script syntax validation via 'bash -n' is possible but no style or quality checking is configured.

testing.notes: No test infrastructure exists. This is a utility script repository with no automated tests. The script is designed to interact with OpenShift clusters running KServe and cannot be tested without cluster access.

ci.notes: No CI/CD configuration found. No GitHub Actions workflows, Tekton pipelines, or other automated checks. The repository appears to be in early development - the main branch only contains LICENSE, while the 'add-initial-files' branch contains the actual scripts but has not been merged.

build.notes: No build system required. Scripts are executed directly. External dependencies (oc, yq, jq) must be installed on the system but there is no package manager or dependency installation mechanism.

conventions.notes: Standard bash scripting conventions. Shebang: #!/bin/bash. No specific coding standards or style guide documented.

lint_commands.notes: Syntax check passed. This only validates bash syntax, not style or quality.

test_commands.notes: Script executes successfully and displays version 1.0.0
Prerequisite check runs successfully and correctly identifies missing 'oc' CLI. Script cannot proceed without OpenShift cluster access.

container_recipe.notes: The convert.sh script requires 'oc' (OpenShift CLI) which is not easily installable in a standard container. The script is designed to run against a live OpenShift cluster with KServe installed. Syntax validation and basic execution checks work, but actual functionality testing requires cluster infrastructure. Note: The bash:5 image uses /usr/local/bin/bash instead of /bin/bash.

results.notes: Successfully installed jq and supporting tools in Alpine-based bash container

validation.summary: Syntax validation OK, script execution OK (--version, --help), prerequisite checks OK. Script requires OpenShift cluster with KServe - cannot test actual conversion functionality without infrastructure.

gaps: No test infrastructure - no unit tests, integration tests, or test framework

No linting configuration - no shellcheck or other shell script quality tools

Main branch contains only LICENSE file - actual code is on 'add-initial-files' branch which has not been merged
No documentation on how to test changes to the scripts
Dependency installation is manual - no package.json, requirements.txt, or similar
No version control or release process documented
